note_data.title: Theory of Quadratic Equations
keyPoints: A quadratic equation is an equation of the form ax² + bx + c = 0, where a ≠ 0
The discriminant Δ = b² - 4ac determines the nature of roots
If Δ > 0, roots are real and distinct; if Δ = 0, roots are real and equal; if Δ < 0, roots are complex
Sum of roots = -b/a and Product of roots = c/a
A quadratic equation can be solved by factorization, completing the square, or using the quadratic formula
The graph of a quadratic function is a parabola
Maximum and minimum values occur at vertex x = -b/2a

definitions.definition: An equation of the second degree in one variable is called a quadratic equation. The general form is ax² + bx + c = 0, where a, b, c are real numbers and a ≠ 0.
The expression b² - 4ac is called the discriminant of the quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0. It is denoted by Δ (Delta).
The values of the variable which satisfy the quadratic equation are called the roots or solutions of the equation.
The character of roots (real, equal, distinct, or complex) is determined by the value of discriminant.

importantQuestions.question: Find the discriminant and nature of roots of 2x² - 5x + 3 = 0
importantQuestions.answer: Here a = 2, b = -5, c = 3. Discriminant Δ = b² - 4ac = (-5)² - 4(2)(3) = 25 - 24 = 1 > 0. Since Δ > 0, roots are real and distinct.

importantQuestions.question: Solve the equation x² - 6x + 9 = 0 by factorization method and verify the answer using sum and product of roots.
importantQuestions.answer: x² - 6x + 9 = 0. Factoring: (x - 3)(x - 3) = 0. Therefore x = 3, 3 (equal roots). Verification: Sum of roots = 3 + 3 = 6 = -(-6)/1 = 6 ✓. Product of roots = 3 × 3 = 9 = 9/1 = 9 ✓

mcqs.question: If the roots of ax² + bx + c = 0 are α and β, then α + β equals:

commonMistakes: Students forget that a ≠ 0 in the definition of quadratic equation
Wrong calculation of discriminant - forgetting the negative sign in -4ac
Confusing sum and product of roots formulas
Not simplifying the quadratic formula completely
Forgetting to write ± in the quadratic formula
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question in every BSEK paper
Always write the quadratic formula before applying it
Show discriminant calculation step by step for full marks
Practice factorization problems as they appear frequently
formulas: Quadratic equation from roots: x² - (α + β)x + αβ = 0
note_data.keyDiagram: Graph of quadratic function showing parabola opening upward (a > 0) or downward (a < 0), vertex at (-b/2a, f(-b/2a)), axis of symmetry x = -b/2a, and x-intercepts as roots of equation
